frames.name: Technical Innovation Focus
frames.description: Emphasizes the technical specifications and engineering achievements of the new chips, particularly the 'Fusion Architecture' and performance improvements
frames.emphasis: CPU/GPU performance gains, architectural innovations, and technical capabilities for professional users

frames.name: Price Increase Concern
frames.description: Highlights the significant price increases across the MacBook lineup while acknowledging the increased storage and performance
frames.emphasis: Cost implications for consumers and the trade-offs between price and enhanced specifications

frames.name: AI-First Strategy
frames.description: Positions the announcements as part of Apple's broader pivot toward AI capabilities and local processing
frames.emphasis: Strategic business implications, AI workload performance, and competitive positioning against cloud-based solutions

frames.name: Professional User Targeting
frames.description: Focuses on specific professional use cases and workflows that benefit from the new hardware
frames.emphasis: Target audiences like 3D animators, AI researchers, data modelers, and app developers

frames.name: Incremental Upgrade Assessment
frames.description: Evaluates whether the changes justify upgrading from previous generations
frames.emphasis: Value proposition for existing users and upgrade recommendations

outlierPerspectives: CNBC's business context linking Mac sales decline (7% drop to $8.39 billion) to the timing of these announcements
CNBC's analysis of memory supply constraints affecting pricing due to AI data center demand
Ars Technica's technical skepticism noting that multi-die designs 'aren't novel' as Intel and AMD have used similar approaches
CNBC's strategic framing of this as part of a broader week of announcements targeting multiple price points
missingContext: Competitive benchmarks against Intel, AMD, or other ARM-based processors
